sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Otitis media with effusion in under 12s — Recommendations
sources.version: NICE guideline NG233; minor correction March 2024
sources.jurisdiction: England, United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE paediatric, audiology, and ENT adaptation
sources.population: Children under 12 years with suspected or confirmed otitis media with effusion and their parents or carers.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, paediatric, audiology, and ENT recognition, assessment, reassessment, and follow-up.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for paediatric OME-associated hearing, communication, balance, mouth-breathing and adenoid context; otoscopy, hearing testing, tympanometry, reassessment, and post-grommet review. It does not support acute otitis media, adults, automatic treatment, or a procedure decision.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.2.7 — concerns, impact, recognition, and formal assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports concerns and functional impact, hearing difficulty, speech or behaviour context, balance, URTI and AOM history, mouth breathing, adenoid context, otoscopy, developmental assessment, hearing testing, and tympanometry.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.3 — reassessment and changing needs
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-arranged reassessment, interval hearing impact, changing needs, and documented questions without generating an interval or intervention.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.5 — surgical discussion and post-grommet follow-up
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording clinician-completed discussion, post-grommet hearing review, otorrhoea context, and follow-up. Procedure, antibiotic, and treatment recommendations are not mapped.
